tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi, I wanted to check if there's any existing information stored about our constituents' preferences or past community interactions. Before I add new details, I want to see what's already been recorded so I don't duplicate anything.
I'll search for any stored information about constituent preferences and community interactions.

The search returned no results—there doesn't appear to be any stored information about constituent preferences or past community interactions yet. You're clear to add new details without worrying about duplicates.

Would you like me to show all existing memories (if any) just to confirm the storage is empty?

Yes, that works for me. Actually, I do have some important information to store right now. Last week at the town hall, several residents brought up concerns about the lack of sidewalk accessibility on Maple Street near the elementary school - parents are worried about kids walking to school safely. I'd like to record that so we can follow up with the public works department. Can you help me save that information?
I'll store that information for you right now.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Town hall concern (last week): Several residents raised concerns about lack of sidewalk accessibility on Maple Street near the elementary school. Parents are worried about children's safety walking to school. Action needed: Follow up with public works department."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Town hall concern (last week): Several residents raised concerns about lack of sidewalk accessibility on Maple Street near the elementary school. Parents are worried about children's safety walking to school. Action needed: Follow up with public works department."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've stored the Maple Street sidewalk accessibility concern. The memory includes:

- **Issue**: Lack of sidewalk accessibility near the elementary school
- **Concern**: Parents worried about children's safety walking to school
- **Location**: Maple Street
- **Source**: Town hall (last week)
- **Next step**: Follow up with public works department

The record is saved with ID `m2` for future reference. Would you like to add any other community concerns or details?
Great, thanks for saving that. Could you show me all the memories that are stored now so I can confirm everything's there?

{"memories": [{"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Town hall concern (last week): Several residents raised concerns about lack of sidewalk accessibility on Maple Street near the elementary school. Parents are worried about children's safety walking to school. Action needed: Follow up with public works department."}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_memories

messages.content:
Here's what's currently stored:

| ID | Memory |
|---|---|
| **m2** | Town hall concern (last week): Several residents raised concerns about lack of sidewalk accessibility on Maple Street near the elementary school. Parents are worried about children's safety walking to school. Action needed: Follow up with public works department. |

Only the one memory we just added is present, so you're all set. Let me know if you'd like to add more details or need help with anything else!

Now that I've confirmed the memory is stored, I want to make sure we keep our records clean and up to date. Is there anything in the stored memories that might be outdated or incorrect that we should remove or update?

Based on the stored memories, there's only one record (m2), and it appears to be current and accurate:

- **It's recent** - from last week's town hall
- **The information is clear** - it documents the concern, location, and required action
- **It's still relevant** - the sidewalk accessibility issue is an ongoing concern that needs follow-up

There's nothing outdated or incorrect to remove or update. The memory is fresh and ready for your follow-up with the public works department.

Would you like to add any additional context or is there anything else I can help with?
